title: Carry the tenant id on every data access
description: Include the authenticated tenant in the filter of every query, read and write, so no operation can ever touch another tenant's rows.
body: Use when writing or reviewing any data-access code in a pooled multi-tenant system. Do not fetch by id alone and trust that the id belongs to the caller's tenant. Actions: derive the tenant from the authenticated principal, never request input; add the tenant predicate to every read and write at the query layer; centralize it so it cannot be forgotten. Validation signals include a negative test where tenant A cannot reach tenant B's row by id. Risk boundary: a query missing the tenant predicate is a cross-tenant leak and a high-severity defect, so treat any such gap as blocking and escalate.
